title: SPARTA Crypto-D1 Operator Missing Items Checklist v1
research_objective: Provide a fillable, schema-validated checklist that turns the Bundle 21 readiness gate's 16 missing-item verdict into a per-item operator template. This checklist authorizes nothing operational: no data fetch, no exchange connection, no QA against real data, no backtest, no paper/live trading. It records operator progress only. NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA is the honest default. Specification completeness is not data readiness.
overall_readiness_status_override_reason: Dataset frozen and fees filled; checklist items 5–13 complete; TOS evidence item 4, QA items 14–15, and baseline item 16 still pending.

items.name: Operator acquire-decision
items.description: An explicit operator decision to acquire Crypto-D1 data this cycle. A research decision, not a default. Reference the dated record (memo, decisions.md append, or signed checklist) that captures the decision.
items.next_action: Operator records a dated acquire-decision in the project log or this field.

items.name: Source class
items.description: Chosen source class per Bundle 18: one of A_exchange_public_historical_archives, B_exchange_public_apis (WATCH future-only, operator-side), C_paid_market_data_vendors (PREFERRED), D_existing_local_OHLCV_files. Classes E (web-scraped) and F (manual/screenshots) are REJECTED for evidence; never choose E or F.
items.next_action: Operator picks one of A/B/C/D and records the choice with a one-line justification.

items.name: Source name
items.description: Exact vendor / venue / archive identity (e.g., 'Vendor X paid daily-OHLCV CSV export, Y product tier'). Anonymous provenance is invalid per Bundle 12.
items.next_action: Operator names the concrete source for the chosen class.

items.name: License/TOS confirmation
items.description: Explicit pointer to the license / TOS that permits research use of the data. Recorded URL, document filename, or vendor confirmation reference.
items.next_action: Operator reviews license/TOS and records the reference; if license is restrictive or unclear, mark BLOCKED with reason.

items.name: BTC/ETH/SOL spot 1D dataset
items.description: A concrete dataset declaring BTC, ETH, and SOL on spot only at 1d timeframe. Perps / dated futures / options / leveraged tokens / margin spot / synthetic instruments are forbidden. Intraday is out of scope.
items.operator_answer: BTC, ETH, SOL spot daily (1d) dataset frozen at CRYPTO_D1_SPOT_BTC_ETH_SOL_V001/V001. Spot only; no perps / futures / options / leverage; daily bars only.
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — manifest.json confirms market_type=spot, timeframe=1d, assets BTC/ETH/SOL.
items.next_action: Complete — spot-only daily BTC/ETH/SOL dataset frozen.

items.name: Date range
items.description: ISO-8601 UTC time_start and time_end. The window must cover the dataset's full intended IS/OOS span without backfill peeking.
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — ISO date range recorded in manifest.json (timezone UTC).

items.name: dataset_id
items.description: Identifier in the form CRYPTO_D1_SPOT_<SYMBOLS_TAG>_V<NNN> (e.g., CRYPTO_D1_SPOT_BTC_ETH_SOL_V001).
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — dataset_id matches the required pattern.

items.name: dataset_version
items.description: Immutable per-freeze version. Remediation requires a NEW version, never in-place edit.
items.operator_answer: V001 — first immutable freeze of this dataset_id.
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — monotonic dataset_version V001 recorded in manifest.json.

items.name: Storage path
items.description: Concrete storage path under data/crypto_d1_research/<dataset_id>/<dataset_version>/. Created only by operator action; SPARTA never creates it.
items.operator_answer: data/crypto_d1_research/CRYPTO_D1_SPOT_BTC_ETH_SOL_V001/V001/ created by operator manual Kraken intake.
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — storage path exists on disk with the frozen dataset files.
items.next_action: Complete — storage path created by operator.

items.name: fees.json
items.description: fees.json with per-venue taker fee + slippage (optional spread_proxy). No QA_PASS without taker fee AND slippage declared.
items.operator_answer: fees.json filled: Kraken Pro spot, taker_fee_bps=40, slippage_bps=10, spread_proxy_bps=10 (status FILLED_PENDING_QA; conservative low-volume baseline).
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — taker fee + slippage declared. Assumptions remain subject to QA-harness review; this item only requires declaration, not QA.
items.next_action: Complete — fees.json declares taker fee + slippage.

items.name: manifest.json
items.description: Per-dataset manifest.json passing tools/crypto_d1_dataset_manifest_check.py. Pins protocol/data_contract/manifest versions; declares lane/market/timeframe/missing_day_policy/etc.
items.operator_answer: manifest.json present; pins protocol_version=crypto_d1_protocol_v1, data_contract_version=crypto_d1_data_contract_v1, manifest_version=crypto_d1_dataset_manifest_v1; declares lane/market/timeframe/missing_day_policy.

items.name: CHECKSUMS.txt
items.description: One sha256_hex<TAB or 2-space>filename line per dataset file. The QA runtime tool verifies sha256 against this file at runtime.
items.operator_answer: CHECKSUMS.txt records one sha256 line each for daily_ohlcv.csv, fees.json, and manifest.json.
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — sha256 line present per frozen dataset file.

items.name: FREEZE_RECORD.txt
items.description: key:value lines including freeze_timestamp_utc, operator (name/label), and protocol+data_contract+dataset_manifest+qa_freeze_spec+backtest_plan+authorization_plan version pins (>=3 of the 6 pins required by Bundle 19).
items.operator_answer: FREEZE_RECORD.txt records freeze_timestamp 2026-06-02T21:39:35Z, operator 'Mahmoud Cherif', and 3 version pins (protocol_version, data_contract_version, manifest_version).
items.reviewer_notes: Approved 2026-06-02 — timestamp + operator + 3 version pins present (>=3 of 6 required). Note: kraken_tos_evidence_reference in this file is still a placeholder, tracked separately under item 4.
items.next_action: Complete — freeze record present with timestamp, operator, and version pins.

items.name: QA run / qa_report.json
items.description: A QA run via tools/crypto_d1_data_qa_runtime_tool.py run --dataset-dir <PATH> --out-dir <PATH> producing qa_report.json + qa_report.md outside the dataset directory.
items.next_action: Operator runs the QA tool against the frozen dataset and records the qa_report.json path.

items.name: QA_PASS or accepted QA_WARN
items.description: qa_status in qa_report.json is QA_PASS, OR QA_WARN with an explicit operator-acceptance memo attached. QA_FAIL and QA_BLOCKED do not satisfy this item; remediation requires a NEW dataset_version.
items.next_action: Operator reviews qa_report.json and either accepts QA_PASS, attaches a QA_WARN acceptance memo, or moves to a new dataset_version.

items.name: Baseline backtest output
items.description: A separately authorized baseline backtest run via tools/crypto_d1_backtest_runner.py producing per-strategy report output. This checklist does NOT authorize the backtest; it only records whether the output exists.
items.next_action: After a separately authorized backtest bundle approves it, operator runs the backtest runner and records the report output path.

required_artifact_checks: Every item has the 9 required fields (number, name, status, operator_answer, evidence_path, reviewer_notes, approval_status, blocking_reason, next_action).
Every item's status is one of MISSING / COMPLETE / BLOCKED / NOT_APPLICABLE.
Every item's approval_status is one of PENDING / APPROVED / REJECTED / WITHDRAWN.
Default state pins every item at MISSING with empty operator_answer + evidence_path + reviewer_notes + blocking_reason and approval_status=PENDING.
Anti-fake-completion: status=COMPLETE requires non-empty evidence_path AND approval_status=APPROVED.
Consistency: if any item is COMPLETE, overall_readiness_status must not remain 'NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA' unless overall_readiness_status_override_reason explicitly documents why.
NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA is the honest default. Specification completeness is not data readiness.
allowed_next_steps: Operator step (NOT a bundle): operator follows the Bundle 20 runbook, fills in this checklist item-by-item, and attaches evidence per item. Each item moves from MISSING -> COMPLETE only with non-empty evidence_path + approval_status=APPROVED.
After any operator fill, re-run python tools/crypto_d1_operator_missing_items_checklist_check.py validate to catch fake-completion or inconsistency.
After items 1-13 are COMPLETE, operator runs the Bundle 19 QA tool to populate item 14 with a qa_report.json path.
After QA_PASS or accepted QA_WARN, item 15 is COMPLETE; a separately authorized backtest bundle is then required before item 16 can move to COMPLETE.
Alternative: keep Crypto-D1 in WATCH; the foundation needs no further spec work.
forbidden_next_steps: Do NOT fetch data of any kind from this checklist.
Do NOT connect to any exchange from this checklist.
Do NOT run any QA against real data from this checklist.
Do NOT run any backtest from this checklist.
Do NOT create data/crypto_d1_research/ from this checklist.
Do NOT mark any item COMPLETE without non-empty evidence_path AND approval_status=APPROVED.
Do NOT set overall_readiness_status to a higher lifecycle value without all required items COMPLETE and a recorded override_reason if any item remains BLOCKED.
Do NOT place any order anywhere.
Do NOT enable live or micro-live trading.
Do NOT install any credentials / API keys / .env / private keys.
Do NOT install any scheduler / cron / daemon.
Do NOT mark the Crypto-D1 lane ACTIVE or STRONG.
Do NOT claim profitability, edge, or live-readiness.

trading_gate.name: Future paper / live / micro-live trading gate
trading_gate.note: This checklist does NOT authorize trading and never will. A separate future authorization stack would be required, and even then only after multi-bundle evidence exists. QA_PASS does not authorize live trading. QA_PASS does not authorize paper trading.

lane_recommendation.rationale: The Bundle 21 readiness gate reports NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA. This checklist hands the operator a fillable template for the 16 missing items; it adds no automation, fetches no data, and authorizes no operational step. The next move is OPERATOR-DRIVEN.

safety_boundaries: No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No paper-order execution. No order placement.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this checklist's runtime.
No data fetch in this bundle. No QA run against real data in this bundle. No backtest in this bundle.
Do not create data/crypto_d1_research/. Do not modify paper / live execution files.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence.
NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_DATA is the honest default. Specification completeness is not data readiness. Crypto-D1 remains WATCH / MIXED.
no_profit_claim_policy: A checklist is not strategy validation.
Specification completeness is not data readiness.
QA_PASS does not authorize automatic backtesting.
QA_PASS does not authorize paper trading.
QA_PASS does not authorize live trading.
No real data has entered SPARTA.
A good historical chart does not imply future returns.
